reasoning:
The baseline rate for protests in Azerbaijan is approx 1.167 per 30 days, meaning we need 2 or more protests in any 30-day window to resolve YES. Historically, this corresponds to a roughly 33% chance under a Poisson distribution. 
For the June 29 resolution date, we are in the last week of the 30-day window (May 30 to June 28) with only 1 known protest (June 1 crackdown on Elgun protests). We would need at least one more event in the remaining days, or an unrecorded one to trigger a YES, placing the probability lower than the base rate. For future dates, the expected probability rests near the historic base rate (33-35%), but with increasing uncertainty (closer to 50%) for the highly distant late-2025 resolution dates, since political dynamics could shift or baseline volatility increases.
